LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS GURBAKSH CHAHAL AND GRAVITY4, INC.'S MOTION TO DISQUALIFY PLAINTIFFS COUNSEL OF RECORD IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT MADE A SUFFICIENT SHOWING THAT THEIR PRIVILEGED MATERIALS HAVE BEEN OR ARE LIKELY TO BE USED BY PLAINTIFF ALI AL-ANSARI OR HIS COUNSEL TO WARRANT THE STRONG REMEDY OF DISQUALIFICATION. NOTWITHSTANDING THE PARTIES' STRONG NEGATIVE FEELINGS TOWARD EACH OTHER, THERE IS NO REASON TO BELIEVE AT THIS POINT THAT THE INCLUSION OF THE ASSERTEDLY PRIVILEGED EMAILS IN MR. AL-ANSARI'S PAPERS IN OPPOSITION TO THE PERSONAL JURISDICTION MOTION WAS ANYTHING OTHER THAN INADVERTENT. HOWEVER, TO ALLAY DEFENDANTS' LEGITIMATE CONCERNS ABOUT OTHER ASSERTEDLY PRIVILEGED MATERIALS THAT MAY BE IN THE POSSESSION OF MR. AL-ANSARI AND/OR HIS COUNSEL, THE COURT STRONGLY ENCOURAGES (BUT DOES NOT ORDER IN THE ABSENCE OF A MOTION) THE PARTIES TO WORK COOPERATIVELY TO ADDRESS THIS ISSUE IMMEDIATELY. AMONG OTHER POSSIBILITIES, THE PARTIES MAY WISH TO JOINTLY RETAIN A NEUTRAL THIRD PARTY TO TAKE POSSESSION OF THE DEVICES THAT MR. AL-ANSARI HAD WHEN HE WORKED AT GRAVITY4 AND, PER AN AGREED PROTOCOL, INVENTORY SOME OR ALL OF THE MATTERS THEREIN, AND IDENTIFY ANY PRESUMPTIVELY PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS THAT SHOULD BE RETURNED TO DEFENDANTS WITHOUT PLAINTIFF OR HIS COUNSEL RETAINING ANY COPIES. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: LAURA REDING, CSR NO. 9711, E-MAIL: LREDING@SFTC.ORG

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-01-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Adrian G. Driscoll, Esq. of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney, T:415.788.1900, for defendant/moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS GRAVITY 4, INC. AND GURBAKSH CHAHAL'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as follows:

Defendants Gravity4, Inc. and Gurbaksh Chahal's demurrer to the second, seventh, eighth and ninth causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Ali Al- Ansari is sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh and eight causes of action alleged against Mr. Chalal and overruled as to all other causes of action. The first amended complaint adequately alleges a claim for religious discrimination against Gravity 4. The allegations that Mr. Chahal used the derogatory epithets "terrorist" and "ISIS" support a reasonable inference that they were based on Mr. Al-Ansari's perceived Islamic religion and the allegation that Mr. Al-Ansari's was threatened to be fired if he did not stop complaining supports the element that religion was a substantial factor in the termination of Mr. Al-Ansari's employment. The first amended complaint adequately alleges claims for breach of contract and the breach of the implied covenant against Gravity 4, but not against Mr. Chalal. Liberally construed, the implied covenant claim is not duplicative of the breach of contract claim. Because Mr. Al-Ansari has not identified any facts supporting a contractual relationship he had with Mr. Chalal, there is no basis to give leave to amend to Mr. Al-Ansari to allege contract and implied covenant claims against Mr. Chalal. The first amended complaint also adequately alleges a Labor Code 970 claim against both defendants. The allegations that Mr. Chahal offered Mr. Al-Ansari a written contract omitting the equity that had been promised to Mr. Al-Ansari while he was residing in Britain suffices for this claim.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.

Appearances:

Attorney, James A. Lassart of the law offices of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney (415-788-1900) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

This action is passed and re-called due to a late arrival.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. Court admonishes plaintiff counsel as to the seriousness of this matter and the importance of following the court's suggestions.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS GURBAKSH CHAHAL and GRAVITY4, INC.'S MOTION To Disqualify Plaintiffs Counsel Of Record is DENIED. Defendants have not made a sufficient showing that their privileged materials have been or are likely to be used by plaintiff Ali Al-Ansari or his counsel to warrant the strong remedy of disqualification. Notwithstanding the parties' strong negative feelings toward each other, there is no reason to believe at this point that the inclusion of the assertedly privileged emails in Mr. Al-Ansari's papers in opposition to the personal jurisdiction motion was anything other than inadvertent. However, to allay defendants' legitimate concerns about other assertedly privileged materials that may be in the possession of Mr. Al-Ansari and/or his counsel, the court strongly encourages (but does not order in the absence of a motion) the parties to work cooperatively to address this issue immediately. Among other possibilities, the parties may wish to jointly retain a neutral third party to take possession of the devices that Mr. Al-Ansari had when he worked at Gravity4 and, per an agreed protocol, inventory some or all of the matters therein, and identify any presumptively privileged documents that should be returned to defendants without plaintiff or his counsel retaining any copies. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Summons And Complaint On The Basis Of Lack Of Jurisdiction Over Defendants Gravity4 And Gurbaksh S. Chahal And To Dismiss On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Oct-17-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Victoria Gutierrez, Esq. (866-920-1292) of Bohm Law Group for Plaintiff.
Adrian Driscoll, Esq. (415-788-1900) of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeny for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces the basis for the tentative ruling. Defendant Argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GRAVITY4, INC., GURBAKSH CHAHAL's Motion To Quash Summons And Complaint On The Basis Of Lack Of Jurisdiction Over Defendants Gravity4 And Gurbaksh S. Chahal And To Dismiss On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens.

Defendants Gravity4, Inc. and Gurbaksh Chalal's motion to quash summons and complaint and motion to dismiss on the grounds of forum non conveniens are denied. Both defendants purposely availed themselves of the benefits of California and are subject to specific personal jurisdiction in this state for this case. Mr. Al-Ansari's claims arise in part from alleged wrongful conduct committed by Gravity4 and Mr. Chalal in California during Mr. Al-Ansari employment by Gravity4. Specific jurisdiction lies even though some of the alleged wrongful conduct committed by defendant occurred outside California, such as the alleged wrongful termination occurred in Florida. (Yahoo! Inc. v. La Ligue Contre Le Racisme Et L'Antisemitisme (9th Cir. 2006) 433 F.3d 1199, 1207). Based on defendants' substantial prior contacts with California, exercise of personal jurisdiction over them in this state comports with traditional notions of fair play and substantial justice. There is no basis to dismiss this case on the grounds of forum non conveniens. Defendants have not shown that California is a seriously inconvenient forum. Nor do either the private or public factors favor having this case decided in Florida. Gravity4 and Mr. Chahal have failed to show that they would be unreasonably burdened by litigating in California. California has a strong interest in retaining this case since Mr. Al-Ansari's statutory claims are based on California law and California has a substantial interest in regulating the conduct of employment in its jurisdiction.
